agency: New York City Department of Design and Construction (DDC)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted nyc.gov/site/ddc (Akamai + nginx + NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform + Dynatrace + Akamai mPulse + AWS ALB) and read the 'Work With DDC' procurement page, which names the citywide systems DDC solicits through (PASSPort/MOCS, City Record, Checkbook NYC, DDC Anywhere). Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Department of Design and Construction (DDC)' via the Socrata Discovery API and pulled all 4 assets with column schemas.
headline_findings: DDC is a vendor-facing, business-to-government agency — the City's primary capital construction project manager (550+ active projects, $34B+ portfolio) that builds for other agencies. There is no citizen and no citizen transaction anywhere in the domain.

DDC exposes NO API of its own. Its only machine-readable data is 4 NYC Open Data datasets (Socrata SODA), three of them frozen '(Historical)' snapshots of active projects, plus a 4-column directory of awarded contracts.

Every transaction is outsourced to citywide systems DDC does not own: solicitations and vendor prequalification run in PASSPort (managed by MOCS), notices go to the City Record, and contract records live on Checkbook NYC (Comptroller). The one DDC-owned vendor portal (DDC Anywhere) has no API.
The richer live capital data (Capital Commitment Plan / Capital Project Detail Data) is published under OMB/Comptroller labels, not DDC — so even DDC's own portfolio is not fully DDC-published.

Net-new write surface: submit a vendor prequalification / expression of interest (VendorPrequalification) — a B2G transaction that today lives only inside citywide PASSPort. NOTE: there is no citizen write for this agency. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: Only 4 DDC datasets: Active Projects + two '(Historical)' variants and the awarded-contracts directory. SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. The one real, open DDC data surface — thin and mostly historical.
Where ALL DDC solicitations are released and responded to; vendor registration + prequalification. Not DDC-owned; no OpenAPI/JSON.
Solicitation and award notices; vendors register for email alerts.
Public record of DDC/City contracts — but the API belongs to the Comptroller, not DDC.
The one DDC-owned vendor system — design/construction process for contract holders. No machine-readable surface observed.
Content only; Akamai edge, nginx, Dynatrace + mPulse RUM, AWS ALB. No content API.

fruit.name: Active Capital Projects
fruit.notes: Core unit of DDC. Keyed on Project ID; carries Division, Client Agency, Phase, Scope, Dollar Amount, Projected Construction Completion. Two of the three datasets are frozen '(Historical)' snapshots — no live status API.

fruit.name: Awarded Construction Contracts
opendata_match: Directory Of Awarded Construction Contracts j7gw-gcxi (4c)
fruit.notes: Description, Selected Firm, PIN, $Value. The only vendor-facing dataset, just 4 columns. Public contract records also on Checkbook NYC (Comptroller).

fruit.name: Vendors / Firms (designers, engineers, CMs, contractors, consultants)
fruit.notes: DDC has no structured vendor dataset; firm identity is derivable only from the SELECTED FIRM field of awarded contracts. Vendor onboarding/prequalification lives in citywide PASSPort (MOCS).

fruit.name: DDC Divisions (Infrastructure, Public Buildings)
opendata_match: derived from 'Division' column on the Active Projects datasets
fruit.notes: The organizational lens over the portfolio; useful for rolling project counts and dollar totals up by line of business.

fruit.name: Open Solicitations (professional service, construction, not-for-profit)
fruit.notes: All DDC solicitations are released and responded to only through citywide PASSPort (MOCS) and noticed in the City Record. No DDC-owned API or Open Data twin.

fruit.name: Register with the City Record for notices
fruit.notes: Vendors register with the City Record for email notification of matching solicitations — a citywide, non-DDC subscription. Folded into the net-new write as an optional City Record notification opt-in.

fruit.name: Submit a vendor prequalification / expression of interest
fruit.notes: The single most common act for a firm seeking DDC work — register/prequalify and express interest in a solicitation. Today only in citywide PASSPort (MOCS); no DDC-owned API. The net-new WRITE surface for this domain. NOTE: DDC is agency-facing — there is NO citizen write anywhere in the domain; the honest write is B2G vendor onboarding.

meta.platform: NYC.gov shared 'Livesite' platform (Akamai + nginx + Dynatrace + mPulse + AWS ALB) for the informational site; the transaction layer is entirely citywide systems DDC does not own — PASSPort (MOCS), City Record, Checkbook NYC (Comptroller), plus DDC's own API-less DDC Anywhere portal.
meta.netnew: VendorPrequalification — a firm submits a prequalification / expression of interest (optionally against a solicitation, with an optional City Record notice opt-in). A B2G write; DDC is vendor-facing, so there is no citizen write in this domain.
meta.tagline: A vendor-facing agency whose own data is thin and historical and whose transactions all run on citywide systems it doesn't own.
meta.headline: DDC manages a $34B+ capital portfolio but exposes no API of its own — just four mostly-historical Open Data datasets — and outsources every vendor transaction to citywide PASSPort, City Record, and Checkbook.

scorecard_notes.platform: Shared NYC.gov Livesite chassis; no DDC-owned application platform with an API, and the transaction layer is citywide systems (PASSPort/MOCS, City Record, Checkbook).
scorecard_notes.siteApi: No site or content API; nothing machine-readable on nyc.gov/site/ddc.
scorecard_notes.openData: Only 4 Socrata datasets, three of them frozen '(Historical)' snapshots and one a 4-column contract directory — present but thin and stale.
scorecard_notes.coreData: CapitalProject and AwardedContract are modeled and open but only as snapshots; vendor and division are derived; no live status.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: No write surface at all — solicitation, prequalification, and onboarding are citywide PASSPort/MOCS; no citizen write exists.
scorecard_notes.agent: No agent-native surface; no MCP, no structured intake an agent could call.
scorecard_notes.ownership: DDC owns almost none of its surface — reference data is thin and partly published under OMB/Comptroller, and the transaction systems belong to MOCS and the Comptroller.
